Tests if the modulus of negative infinity and infinity is NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the result of the modulus operation between negative infinity and positive infinity. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of `-Infinity % Infinity` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles the modulus operation involving infinite values, which is a part of validating mathematical operations in JavaScript. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on this mathematical edge case.
